name: Cardano Summit 2025
location: Gasometer Schöneberg, Berlin, Germany (Day Zero hackathon/pitch on 11 November at w3.hub Berlin)
attendance: ~800 in-person attendees from 70+ countries, 140 speakers, and 25,000+ online participants
defi_themes: Stablecoins for institutional finance (cross-border payments, payroll, treasury management, trading)
DeFi liquidity expansion as a 2026 Cardano roadmap priority
Real-world asset (RWA) tokenization for enterprise adoption
Tier-1 stablecoin issuance and stablecoin diversity on Cardano (Brale regulated-stablecoin partnership)
Institutional DeFi adoption and traditional-bank 'FOMO' under supportive regulation (MiCA / regulatory alignment)
Centralized exchange (CEX) listings to deepen DeFi liquidity
Zero-knowledge proofs enabling selective disclosure / privacy for on-chain finance
DeFi as an extension of established finance requiring trust, education and institutional alignment

notable_panels.title: Stablecoins & payment rails panel
notable_panels.topic: Stablecoins solving real issues beyond cross-border payments: payroll, treasury management, trading and DeFi applications; 'more optionality now than ever with payments'
notable_panels.speakers: Christian Rau (Mastercard), Kwon Park (Crypto.com), Vanessa Fernandes (Aeonic Digital)

notable_panels.title: RWA tokenization & 2026 DeFi priorities
notable_panels.topic: Real-world asset tokenization, tier-1 stablecoins, improved DeFi liquidity and CEX listings as enterprise-adoption priorities for 2026

notable_panels.title: Toto Finance / tokenized commodities announcement
notable_panels.topic: Initiative integrating Cardano into tokenized commodities infrastructure

notable_panels.title: Brale regulated-stablecoin partnership announcement
notable_panels.topic: Cardano Foundation partnership with Brale to increase regulated stablecoin diversity on the network

notable_speakers.name: Thomas Mayfield
notable_speakers.affiliation: Cardano Foundation (Head of Decentralized Trust and Identity)

key_takeaways: Trust emerged as the headline theme; traditional financial firms exhibited 'blockchain FOMO', with Boston Consulting Group noting roughly two-thirds of bank conversations now center on stablecoins.
Mastercard's Christian Rau argued stablecoins 'solve a real issue' well beyond cross-border payments, extending into payroll, treasury management and DeFi applications.
The Cardano Foundation's 2026 roadmap explicitly prioritizes DeFi liquidity, Web3 integration and real-world asset (RWA) tokenization.
EMURGO framed 2026 priorities around tier-1 stablecoins, deeper DeFi liquidity, more CEX listings and RWA tokenization to drive enterprise adoption.
New DeFi-adjacent partnerships were announced, including Brale (regulated stablecoins on Cardano) and Toto Finance/LCX (tokenized commodities infrastructure).

DeFi was framed as an extension of established finance: scaling requires trust, education, institutional alignment and supportive regulation, and accelerates when major banks see clear economic incentives.
Singapore (5-6 October 2026) was unveiled as the location for the next Cardano Summit.
